title: Podman should mount volumes only once during container start like docker
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**
/kind feature

**Description**
I observe a difference in behavior when a container is created between docker and podman with respect to bind mounted volumes. During the container creation, docker mount all the bind mounted volumes just once, whereas podman does mount, unmount and mount of the volumes. On a container with larger number of volumes, this increases the time required for container to start.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Start a container with bind mounted volumes

2. Check the podman logs, 'journalctl -u podman'

**Describe the results you received:**

**Describe the results you expected:**
The volume should be just mounted once

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.1.1
```

comments.body:
These aren't bind-mounts. That's a volume plugin. Can you provide details on the plugin in use?

Regardless, the current behavior should be correct. Podman uses a mount counter to keep track of containers using the volume. Every container will attempt to mount the volume, but only the first will actually perform the mount, others simply update the counter to indicate the volume is in use. Once all containers have ceased using the volume, the actual unmount is performed. We may log messages about "mounting" and "unmounting" the volume, but this is purely informational, and reflects how we're calling internal APIs.

It is a custom volume driver plugin. 

For every container start, the volume driver plugin gets mount/unmount/mount request, it is not just informational.
Can you provide a full reproducer with your actual podman commands? I cannot reproduce this, I see only one Mount() call in the plugin when I start several containers with the same volume.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I was going to close this for lack of reproduction info, but thinking about it, there is something that Podman does differently from Docker: We do not send the real container ID to the plugin when mounting/unmounting. Podman manages volume state internally, so we don't send a separate mount request for each container that wants to use the volume. Instead, you get one request when the first Podman container using the volume starts, and a subsequent unmount when the last container using the volume stops, all using a pseudo-container ID. Podman manages all the rest internally.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we never got a reproducer and this conversation has gone cold, I am going to close. Reopen if more information is needed.
